entries.speaker: torren
lines: The spirits went quiet an hour ago. Not silent —
quiet. Like they're holding their breath.

entries.speaker: torren
lines: Whatever's waiting at the Convergence, it's been
waiting a long time. Longer than the spirits. Longer than the
stones. It's patient.

entries.speaker: lira
lines: I've been thinking about something I never finished
building. A housing design — ley-integrated, self-sustaining. I
had the schematics done before the siege.

entries.speaker: lira
lines: If we make it back, I'm going to build it. For real
this time.

entries.speaker: sable
lines: You know what's funny? I spent my whole life avoiding
situations exactly like this. Dark places, impossible odds, no
exit plan.

entries.speaker: sable
lines: Turns out I'm better at this than the other stuff.

entries.speaker: maren
lines: I've seen this story before. In the old texts. Every
age has had a group like us. People who walked toward the
Convergence knowing what it cost.

entries.speaker: maren
lines: This time might be different.

entries.speaker: edren
lines: We're here. We're together. That still means
something.
